meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome can't collaborate. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Antigravity Project Template to Solve Collaboration
content.seo_description: Learn how to use the Antigravity project template to overcome collaboration silos. A high-impact, intermediate guide for freelancers using Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Collaboration silos kill velocity. By standardizing your architecture with the Antigravity project template, you create a shared mental model for your codebase. Use Claude Code to enforce this structure across your projects, ensuring that every file, service, and environment configuration is predictable, scalable, and instantly understandable by any collaborator.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking, the client is waiting, and your codebase has become a monolithic fortress of solitude. You’ve reached the point where scaling your output feels impossible because every new feature requires a week of context-switching or manual hand-holding. The friction of collaboration isn't just a technical debt; it's a bottleneck on your revenue.

But what if your project structure acted as its own documentation? By leveraging the Antigravity project template, you move away from chaotic, bespoke file structures toward a standardized, high-velocity framework. This isn't just about organizing folders; it's about building a repeatable system that allows you to swap in collaborators or AI agents without missing a beat.

steps.title: Initialize the Standardized Scaffold
steps.description: Establish a base repository that enforces directory boundaries and configuration standards, preventing the 'wild west' development style that makes collaboration impossible.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, modular project scaffold that separates core business logic, API interfaces, and infrastructure configuration using the Antigravity standard template.

steps.title: Define the Shared Context Layer
steps.description: Create a centralized 'context.md' that acts as the source of truth for your architecture, ensuring all agents and collaborators align on the same design patterns.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a context.md file that outlines the project architecture, naming conventions, and state management rules for this specific Antigravity project.

steps.title: Implement Type-Safe Contracts
steps.description: Collaboration fails when interfaces change unexpectedly. Use TypeScript or strict schema definitions to ensure your components communicate reliably.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate strict interface definitions for all service-to-service communication layers to ensure type safety across the entire codebase.

steps.title: Automate Documentation Synthesis
steps.description: Use AI to keep your documentation in sync with your code changes, eliminating the drift that usually happens during rapid development cycles.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure an automated workflow that scans the codebase and updates the README.md and API documentation whenever a core change is pushed.

steps.title: Configure Collaborative Access Control
steps.description: Set up scoped environment variables and access patterns to allow collaborators to contribute without compromising the integrity of your production secrets.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Set up an environment configuration template that enforces secure, role-based access for local development environments.

pro_tips: Treat your project structure as a product: version control your folder templates just like your application code.
Use Claude Code to perform 'arch-linting'—ask it to identify any files that deviate from your established pattern.
Always include a 'HOW_TO_CONTRIBUTE.md' file that is generated by AI based on your current project setup.
Prioritize 'declarative configuration' over imperative scripts to make the codebase easier for humans and agents to parse.
Use git hooks to trigger Claude Code analysis on every commit to catch architectural drift before it becomes a merge conflict.
content.next_steps: Once your template is live, perform a 'dry run' by inviting a peer to add a single feature using only your generated documentation. If they can complete it without asking you a question, your system is optimized for scale.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting your own file system and start building with the velocity of a senior team. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
